hypothesis: breadth field appended to /api/market/snapshot; old 4 panels unchanged; F6 stale-lock intact
findings: mcc first non-None at 2026-02-10 (39-day slow EMA warmup as expected)
universe_size=1913, excluded_count={etf:347, warrant:58, watch_list:61} (P1 filter intact)
stale=false (F6 lock verified — breadth compute succeeded so no reason to flip)
leaderboards keys {gainers, losers, amount, volume_ratio} intact (backward-compat)

hypothesis: second call within 24h cache TTL should be much faster (breadth_prices + breadth_taiex cache hit)
findings: remaining 11s comes from other snapshot fetchers (universe 5s TTL live tick + sector/mv/watch_list synchronous cascade)

hypothesis: design v2 §8.1 assumed FinMind TaiwanStockPrice without data_id returns full-window rows; probe was deferred to impl phase
findings: First curl (before fix) returned ad_line_series=[], mcclellan_series=[] despite payload shape being correct
Root cause: FinMind TaiwanStockPrice **without data_id** ignores end_date and returns only start_date's rows (48001 rows all dated 2025-12-15 in the stale cache)
Phase 6 finding routed as (b): SC-4 covered by design but assumption invalid → Phase 1 amend + Phase 3 impl fix (SC-4.phase_1 += 1, SC-4.phase_3 += 1)
Trade-off: cold-fetch cost went from ~3s (broken but fast) to ~257s (correct but slow); mitigated by 24h cache TTL (subsequent cost ~11s)
